channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — The Chinese government placed sanctions on seven companies on Friday in response to recent U.S. announcements of military sales and aid to Taiwan, the self-governing island that China claims as part of its territory. The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Blue state's experiment collapses after a decade and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jackpot for Mega Millions over $1 billion after no numbers match on Christmas Eve
item.description: The estimated jackpot for Friday is $1.15 billion, potentially the fifth-largest jackpot in the game's history.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canadian ministers head to Palm Beach for talks with incoming Trump administration
item.description: Two top Canadian Cabinet ministers are visiting Palm Beach to talk border security and trade with officials in the incoming Donald Trump administration as Trump threatens tariffs on all Canadian products.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Retailers, chains that announced closures in 2024
item.description: It was another tough year for restaurants and traditional brick-and-mortar retailers. Several national chains announced closures and bankruptcies in 2024 – not to mention the local small businesses that quietly shuttered around the country.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC cab driver who plunged into crowd of pedestrians was experiencing medical emergency, police say
item.description: The taxi driver who plowed into a group of pedestrians in a busy shopping district in New York City on Christmas Day was experiencing a medical emergency, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-Sen. Bob Menendez, citing 'emotional toll,' seeks sentencing delay in wake of wife's trial
item.description: Former U.S. Sen. Bob Menendez is asking a federal judge to delay his end-of-January sentencing on bribery charges, saying his family would suffer a “tremendous emotional toll” if the New Jersey Democrat was sentenced during his wife's trial.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Cats can get sick with bird flu. Here's how to protect them
item.description: The death of an Oregon house cat from bird flu and a pet food recall are raising questions about how people can protect their pets.
item.dc:creator: Carla K. Johnson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Investigation is underway in Kazakhstan plane crash
item.description: U.S. officials say Russia could be behind a passenger plane crash in Kazakhstan that left dozens dead Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Biertempfel, KTLA 5

item.title: US census data shows how Middle Eastern and Hispanic residents differ in racial identity
item.description: As the U.S. Census Bureau gets public feedback about how it should tally people into new race and ethnicity groups, the agency has released new research reflecting how U.S. residents from different backgrounds regard their racial and ethnic identities. Earlier this year, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California residents on edge as high surf and flooding threats persist on Christmas Eve
item.description: SANTA CRUZ, Calif. (AP) — Persistent high surf and flooding threats along California’s coast had residents on high alert a day after a major storm was blamed for one man’s death and the partial collapse of a pier, which propelled three people into the Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA MENDOZA and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Homeland Security launches internal AI chatbot for 19,000 staff
item.description: The tool, called DHSChat, will be available to approximately 19,000 employees at DHS headquarters and select pilot users across ten operating agencies.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Medical examiner says a Hawaii crime boss died of a drug overdose while in federal detention
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — A Hawaii crime boss who died in federal detention this month was killed by an opioid overdose, Honolulu’s medical examiner said Tuesday. Michael Miske, 50, died of “toxicity of fentanyl and para-fluorofentanyl,” the medical examiner’s office said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dolly Parton's Imagination Library seeks New York state expansion
item.description: Advocates are asking Gov. Kathy Hochul for money in the next state budget to make New York an official state for the program.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Richard Perry, record producer behind 'You're So Vain' and other hits, dies at 82
item.description: Richard Perry, a hitmaking record producer with a flair for both standards and contemporary sounds whose many successes included Carly Simon's "You're So Vain," Rod Stewart's "The Great American Songbook" series and a Ringo Starr album featuring all four Beatles, died Tuesday. He was 82.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Illinois dentist gets 15 years for stealing patients' drugs, doing surgeries without proper pain management
item.description: Phillip M. Jensen, 64, was sentenced on Dec. 18, specifically for stealing fentanyl from his patients, injecting them with adulterated drugs and performing surgery without proper pain management.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Holesha, NewsNation

item.title: Stunning photos show lava erupting from Hawaii's Kilauea volcano
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — One of the world's most active volcanoes spewed lava into the air for a second straight day on Tuesday. The eruption of Kilauea volcano on Hawaii's Big Island has stayed within the mountain's summit caldera inside Hawaii Volcanoes National Park. No homes were threatened.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden signs a bill officially making the bald eagle the national bird of the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The bald eagle, a symbol of the power and strength of the United States for more than 240 years, earned an overdue honor on Tuesday: It officially became the country's national bird.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Eggs added to FDA's updated healthy claim food list
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration has added eggs to its updated list of healthy foods along with fish and vegetables
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Walmart illegally opened bank accounts for 1 million drivers, lawsuit alleges
item.description: Walmart and fintech company Branch Messenger are being sued by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau for allegedly forcing more than one million delivery workers to use expensive deposit accounts to access their paychecks.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, Fox 5

item.title: FBI investigating after 'unruly' passenger dies aboard Royal Caribbean cruise ship
item.description: An investigation has been opened by the FBI after a guest aboard a Royal Caribbean cruise ship died shortly after leaving the port of San Pedro in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bill Clinton admitted to hospital with fever
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton was admitted to a Washington hospital on Monday with a fever, his spokesperson said on social media.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Bill Clinton hospitalized with fever but in good spirits, spokesperson says
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton was admitted Monday to Georgetown University Medical Center in Washington after developing a fever.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump again calls to buy Greenland after eyeing Canada and the Panama Canal
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — First it was Canada, then the Panama Canal. Now, Donald Trump again wants Greenland. The president-elect is renewing unsuccessful calls he made during his first term for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: READ: House Ethics report on Gaetz allegations
item.description: The House Ethics Committee on Monday released its report on former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), who has faced allegations of sexual misconduct and illicit drug use, among other violations.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, Fox 5

item.title: Luigi Mangione pleads not guilty to state charges in death of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: The man accused of fatally shooting the CEO of UnitedHealthcare pleaded not guilty on Monday to murder and terror charges in a state case that will run parallel to his federal prosecution.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The new right gathers to celebrate Trump and the splintering of the Republican Party
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — As Washington heaved over the possibility of a partial government shutdown, leading far-right figures gathered with thousands of Donald Trump’s most ardent supporters and, for the most part, gloried in splintering the president-elect’s party.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New York City police apprehend suspect in the death of a woman found on fire in a subway car
item.description: New York City police announced Sunday they have in custody a “person of interest” in the early morning death of a woman who they believe may have fallen asleep on a stationary subway train before being intentionally lit on fire by a man she didn't know.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, ABC 4
